name: Bean, Ham and Pasta Soup
recipeIngredient: 1 cup dried pinto beans, rinsed and drained
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine the beans and enough water to cover by 2 inches. Let stand overnight; drain well.
2. Heat the oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the ham, onion, and celery and cook, stirring often until lightly browned, about 6 minutes.
3. Add the garlic and cook, stirring often, for 1 minute. Add the chicken broth, stirring to scrape up the browned bits on the bottom of the skillet.
4. Transfer the mixture to a 3 1/2-quart slow cooker.
5. Add the drained beans, water, and tomato paste. Cover and slow cook until the beans are tender, 7 to 8 hours on Low or 4 to 5 hours on High.
6. Stir in the pasta, salt, and pepper. Turn the heat to high and cook until the pasta is tender, about 30 to 60 minutes.
7. Using a large spoon, crush enough of the beans against the sides of the slow cooker to reach the desired consistency. Serve immediately.
Serves 6 to 8
